board.title: Queuing Theory
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Queueing theory sits at the intersection of computer science, operations research, and statistics. It's extremely applicable to operating system scheduling, network congestion modeling, and load balancing in distributed systems.

clues.question: Process used to model random, independent customer arrivals over time; independently discovered across multiple scientific fields
Model where future queue states depend only on the current system state; foundational to M/M/1 queue analysis
Continuous-time Markov process where states increase or decrease one step at a time; used to represent populations or waiting lines
Scheduling policy where tasks are served in first-in, first-out order
Theorem where the average number of items in a system depends on arrival rate and time spent in the system; broadly applicable across queueing systems
System where customers move through queues sequentially; commonly used to model production pipelines
Customers switching queues for perceived faster service
Customers leaving a queue after waiting too long
Scheduling policy where shorter tasks are served first; ___ job first
Scheduling policy where tasks are served based on urgency rather than arrival order; ___ queue
Scheduling policy where running tasks can be interrupted by more urgent ones; requires context switching between tasks
Customers avoiding queues because of perceived long wait times
Researcher whose 1909 paper "The Theory of Probabilities and Telephone Conversations" helped establish queueing theory
